1.text: Now I, Mormon, make a record of the things I have both seen and heard, and call it the Book of Mormon.

Around the time that Ammaron hid up the records for the Lord, he came to me. I was about ten years old and was beginning to learn in the manner of my people. Ammaron said to me: I can see that you are a serious child and quick to observe.

Therefore, when you are about twenty-four years old, I want you to remember the things you have observed about this people. When you reach that age, go to the land of Antum, to a hill called Shim. There I have stored all the sacred engravings about this people for the Lord.
You shall take the plates of Nephi for yourself, and leave the rest where they are. You shall write on the plates of Nephi all the things you have observed about this people.
I, Mormon, being a descendant of Nephi (and my father's name was also Mormon), remembered the things Ammaron commanded me.
When I was eleven years old, my father brought me south to the land of Zarahemla.
The entire land was covered with buildings, and the people were almost as numerous as the sand of the sea.
In this year a war began between the Nephites (which included the Nephites, Jacobites, Josephites, and Zoramites) and the Lamanites (which included the Lamanites, Lemuelites, and Ishmaelites).
The Lamanites, Lemuelites, and Ishmaelites were all called Lamanites, and the two sides were the Nephites and the Lamanites.
The war began in the borders of Zarahemla, near the waters of Sidon.
The Nephites gathered a great army of more than thirty thousand. That same year they fought several battles, and the Nephites defeated the Lamanites and killed many of them.
The Lamanites gave up their plans, and peace settled in the land for about four years without any bloodshed.
But wickedness spread across the whole land, so much that the Lord took away his beloved disciples, and miracles and healing stopped because of the people's wickedness.
There were no gifts from the Lord, and the Holy Ghost did not come upon anyone because of their wickedness and unbelief.
I was fifteen years old and somewhat serious-minded, so the Lord visited me, and I experienced and knew the goodness of Jesus.

I tried to preach to this people, but I was not allowed to speak to them, because they had deliberately rebelled against their God. The beloved disciples had been taken away from the land because of the people's wickedness.
I remained among them but was forbidden to preach because of the hardness of their hearts. Because of their hard hearts, the land was cursed for their sake.

The Gadianton robbers among the Lamanites terrorized the land so badly that people began hiding their treasures in the earth. But their possessions became impossible to hold onto, because the Lord had cursed the land so that they could not keep them or find them again.
There were sorceries, witchcraft, and dark magic, and the power of the evil one worked across the entire land, fulfilling all the words of Abinadi and also Samuel the Lamanite.

2.text: In that same year, war broke out again between the Nephites and the Lamanites. Even though I was young, I was large for my age, so the Nephites appointed me as the leader of their armies.
So in my sixteenth year, I went out at the head of a Nephite army against the Lamanites. Three hundred and twenty-six years had now passed away.

In the three hundred and twenty-seventh year, the Lamanites attacked us with such overwhelming force that they terrified my armies. My soldiers refused to fight and began retreating toward the northern lands.

We came to the city of Angola and took control of it, preparing to defend ourselves against the Lamanites. We strengthened the city with all our might, but despite all our fortifications, the Lamanites attacked us and drove us out.
They also drove us out of the land of David.
We marched on and came to the land of Joshua, which was near the western seashore.
We gathered our people together as quickly as possible, trying to unite them into one group.

But the land was filled with robbers and Lamanites. Despite the great destruction hanging over my people, they did not repent of their evil ways. So there was blood and slaughter everywhere across the land, among both the Nephites and the Lamanites. It was total chaos throughout the entire land.

The Lamanites had a king named Aaron, and he came against us with an army of forty-four thousand. I faced him with forty-two thousand soldiers. I defeated him with my army, and he fled before me. All this happened by the time three hundred and thirty years had passed away.

The Nephites began to feel sorry for their sins and started crying out, just as the prophet Samuel had foretold. No one could keep anything they owned because of the thieves, robbers, murderers, and dark magic that filled the land.
So mourning and grief spread throughout the entire land because of these things, especially among the Nephites.

When I, Mormon, saw their grief, mourning, and sorrow before the Lord, my heart began to rejoice within me. I knew of the Lord's mercy and patience, and I hoped he would be merciful to them so they would become a righteous people again.

But my joy was in vain, because their sorrow was not the kind that leads to repentance through the goodness of God. Instead, it was the sorrow of the condemned, because the Lord would not always let them find happiness in sin.
They did not come to Jesus with broken hearts and humble spirits. Instead, they cursed God and wished to die. Yet they still fought for their lives with the sword.

My sorrow returned, and I saw that the day of grace had passed for them, both physically and spiritually. I saw thousands of them cut down in open rebellion against God, piled up like refuse on the face of the land. Three hundred and forty-four years had now passed away.
In the three hundred and forty-fifth year, the Nephites began fleeing before the Lamanites. They were chased until they reached the land of Jashon before they could stop their retreat.

The city of Jashon was near the place where Ammaron had stored the records for the Lord so they would not be destroyed. I had followed Ammaron's instructions and taken the plates of Nephi, and I made a record as Ammaron had directed.

On the plates of Nephi, I wrote a full account of all the wickedness and evil. But on these plates I chose not to give a full account of their wickedness, because a never-ending scene of sin and evil has been before my eyes ever since I was old enough to understand the ways of people.
How terrible it is because of their wickedness! My heart has been filled with sorrow over their wickedness all my life. Nevertheless, I know that I will be raised up at the last day.
In this year, the Nephites were again hunted and driven. We were pushed northward until we came to a land called Shem.
We fortified the city of Shem and gathered as many of our people as possible, hoping we could save them from destruction.
In the three hundred and forty-sixth year, the Lamanites began attacking us again.
I spoke to my people and urged them with great energy to stand bravely before the Lamanites and fight for their wives, children, houses, and homes.
My words stirred them somewhat to action, so that they did not run from the Lamanites but stood bravely against them.
We fought with an army of thirty thousand against an army of fifty thousand. We stood so firm against them that they fled from us.

When they fled, we chased them with our armies and met them again and defeated them. But the strength of the Lord was not with us. We were left to ourselves, and the Spirit of the Lord did not remain with us, so we had become weak like our enemies.

My heart was full of sorrow over this great disaster that had come upon my people because of their wickedness and evil deeds. But we went out against the Lamanites and the robbers of Gadianton until we had reclaimed the lands of our inheritance.

The three hundred and forty-ninth year passed away. In the three hundred and fiftieth year, we made a treaty with the Lamanites and the robbers of Gadianton, dividing the lands of our inheritance between us.
The Lamanites gave us the land northward, all the way to the narrow passage that led into the land southward. We gave the Lamanites all the land southward.

3.text: The Lamanites did not come to battle again for ten more years. During that time, I had my people, the Nephites, preparing their lands and weapons for the next battle.
The Lord said to me: Call out to this people — Repent and come to me, be baptized, and rebuild my church, and you will be spared.

I called out to the people, but it was useless. They did not recognize that it was the Lord who had spared them and given them a chance to repent. They hardened their hearts against the Lord their God.

After this tenth year had passed, making a total of three hundred and sixty years since the coming of Christ, the king of the Lamanites sent me a letter informing me that they were preparing to attack us again.
I had my people gather at the land of Desolation, at a city on the border near the narrow pass that led into the land southward.
There we positioned our armies to stop the Lamanite armies from taking any of our lands. We fortified our position against them with all our strength.
In the three hundred and sixty-first year, the Lamanites came down to the city of Desolation to fight us. That year we defeated them so thoroughly that they returned to their own lands.
In the three hundred and sixty-second year, they came to fight again. We defeated them again and killed a great number of them, and their dead were thrown into the sea.

Because of this great victory, the Nephites began to boast in their own strength. They started swearing before heaven that they would take revenge for the blood of their fellow soldiers who had been killed by their enemies.
They swore by the heavens and by the throne of God that they would go on the offensive against their enemies and wipe them off the face of the land.
I, Mormon, completely refused from that time forward to be a commander and leader of this people, because of their wickedness and depravity.

I had led them despite their wickedness. I had led them into battle many times and had loved them with the love of God that was in me, with all my heart. My soul had been poured out in prayer to God for them all day long. Yet it was without faith, because of the hardness of their hearts.
Three times I had delivered them from the hands of their enemies, and they still had not repented of their sins.

When they had sworn by everything our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ had forbidden them to swear by — that they would go attack their enemies and take revenge for the blood of their fellow soldiers — the voice of the Lord came to me, saying:
Vengeance is mine, and I will repay. Because this people did not repent after I delivered them, they shall be cut off from the face of the earth.

I absolutely refused to go against my enemies. I did exactly as the Lord commanded me. I stood as a silent witness, showing the world the things I saw and heard, according to the promptings of the Spirit that testified of things to come.
Therefore I write to you, Gentiles, and also to you, house of Israel, when the work shall begin and you are preparing to return to the land of your inheritance.
Yes, I write to all the ends of the earth — to you, twelve tribes of Israel, who will be judged by your works by the twelve whom Jesus chose as his disciples in the land of Jerusalem.
I also write to the remnant of this people, who will be judged by the twelve whom Jesus chose in this land. And they will be judged by the other twelve whom Jesus chose in the land of Jerusalem.

The Spirit reveals these things to me, so I write to all of you. I write for this reason: so you may know that you must all stand before the judgment seat of Christ — every soul who belongs to the whole human family of Adam — and you must be judged by your works, whether they are good or evil.

I also write so that you may believe the gospel of Jesus Christ, which you will have among you, and so that the Jews, the covenant people of the Lord, may have another witness besides the one they saw and heard — that Jesus, whom they killed, was the very Christ and the very God.
I wish I could persuade everyone on earth to repent and prepare to stand before the judgment seat of Christ.

4.text: In the three hundred and sixty-third year, the Nephites marched with their armies to attack the Lamanites, going out from the land of Desolation.

The Nephite armies were driven back to the land of Desolation. While they were still exhausted, a fresh Lamanite army attacked them. There was a terrible battle, and the Lamanites captured the city of Desolation, killing many Nephites and taking many prisoners.
The survivors fled and joined the people in the city of Teancum. The city of Teancum was near the seashore and also near the city of Desolation.
It was because the Nephite armies had gone on the offensive against the Lamanites that they began to be defeated. Otherwise, the Lamanites would have had no power over them.
But the judgments of God will catch up with the wicked. It is by the wicked that the wicked are punished, for it is the wicked who stir up the hearts of people to bloodshed.
The Lamanites prepared to attack the city of Teancum.
In the three hundred and sixty-fourth year, the Lamanites came against the city of Teancum, trying to capture it as well.

They were driven back by the Nephites. When the Nephites saw that they had pushed the Lamanites back, they once again boasted of their own strength. They went out in their own power and retook the city of Desolation.
All these things had happened, and thousands had been killed on both sides, among both the Nephites and the Lamanites.

The three hundred and sixty-sixth year passed, and the Lamanites came against the Nephites to battle again. Yet the Nephites still did not repent of the evil they had done but continued in wickedness.

It is impossible to describe with words or to write a complete description of the horrible scene of blood and slaughter among both the Nephites and the Lamanites. Every heart was hardened, and they found pleasure in the constant shedding of blood.
There had never been such great wickedness among all the descendants of Lehi, or even among all the house of Israel, according to the words of the Lord, as there was among this people.
The Lamanites captured the city of Desolation because their numbers were greater than the Nephites'.
They also marched against the city of Teancum and drove out its people. They took many prisoners, both women and children, and offered them as sacrifices to their idol gods.

In the three hundred and sixty-seventh year, the Nephites were so furious that the Lamanites had sacrificed their women and children that they attacked the Lamanites with extreme anger. They defeated the Lamanites and drove them out of their lands.
The Lamanites did not attack the Nephites again until the three hundred and seventy-fifth year.
In that year, they came against the Nephites with all their forces. Their numbers were too great to count.
From this time on, the Nephites never gained power over the Lamanites again but began to be swept away by them like dew before the sun.
The Lamanites attacked the city of Desolation, and an extremely fierce battle was fought in the land of Desolation, in which the Lamanites defeated the Nephites.
The Nephites fled and came to the city of Boaz. There they made a stand against the Lamanites with great boldness, and the Lamanites could not defeat them until they attacked a second time.
When the Lamanites came the second time, the Nephites were defeated and slaughtered in a terrible massacre. Their women and children were again sacrificed to idols.
The Nephites fled again, taking all the people with them from both towns and villages.
Now I, Mormon, seeing that the Lamanites were about to conquer the land, went to the hill Shim and retrieved all the records that Ammaron had hidden for the Lord.

5.text: I went among the Nephites and took back the oath I had made that I would no longer help them. They gave me command of their armies again, because they looked to me as though I could save them from their troubles.

But I was without hope, because I knew the judgments of the Lord that were coming upon them. They did not repent of their sins but fought for their lives without calling on the Being who created them.
The Lamanites attacked us as we retreated to the city of Jordan, but they were driven back and did not capture the city at that time.

They attacked us again, and we held the city. Other cities were also held by the Nephites, and these strongholds blocked the Lamanites from reaching the territory beyond us to destroy the people who lived there.

Whatever lands we had passed through where the people had not been gathered in were destroyed by the Lamanites. Their towns, villages, and cities were burned. And so three hundred and seventy-nine years passed away.

In the three hundred and eightieth year, the Lamanites came against us to battle again. We stood against them bravely, but it was all in vain, for their numbers were so great that they trampled the Nephites underfoot.
We fled again. Those who were faster than the Lamanites escaped, and those who could not outrun the Lamanites were cut down and destroyed.

Now I, Mormon, do not wish to torment your souls by describing the awful scene of blood and slaughter that was laid before my eyes. But I know these things must be made known, and all things that are hidden must be revealed.

Knowledge of these things must reach the remnant of these people and the Gentiles, whom the Lord said would scatter this people and consider them as nothing. So I write a brief summary, not daring to give a full account of what I have seen, because of the commandment I received, and also so you would not have too great sorrow over the wickedness of this people.
I speak these things to their descendants, and also to the Gentiles who care for the house of Israel and recognize where their blessings come from.

I know that such people will grieve over the tragedy of the house of Israel. They will grieve for the destruction of this people. They will grieve that this people did not repent so they could have been embraced in the arms of Jesus.

These things are written to the remnant of the house of Jacob. They are written in this way because God knows that wickedness will not bring the records to them. They are to be hidden up for the Lord, to come forth in his own due time.
This is the commandment I have received: they shall come forth according to the Lord's command, when he sees fit in his wisdom.

They shall go to the unbelieving Jews for this purpose — that they may be persuaded that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of the living God, so that the Father may bring about, through his most Beloved, his great and eternal purpose of restoring the Jews, or all the house of Israel, to the land of their inheritance that the Lord their God has given them, fulfilling his covenant.

And also so that the descendants of this people may more fully believe the gospel, which shall go to them from the Gentiles. For this people shall be scattered and shall become a dark, filthy, and repulsive people, beyond description of anything that has ever been among us or even among the Lamanites, because of their unbelief and idol worship.
The Spirit of the Lord has already stopped working with their ancestors. They are without Christ and God in the world, driven about like chaff before the wind.
They were once a delightful people, and they had Christ as their shepherd. They were led by God the Father himself.
But now they are led about by Satan, like chaff driven before the wind, or like a ship tossed on the waves without a sail, anchor, or anything to steer with. That is exactly what they are like.
The Lord has reserved the blessings they could have received in this land for the Gentiles who will possess it.

But they will be driven and scattered by the Gentiles. After they have been driven and scattered by the Gentiles, the Lord will remember the covenant he made with Abraham and with all the house of Israel.
The Lord will also remember the prayers of the righteous, which have been offered to him on their behalf.
Then, O you Gentiles, how can you stand before the power of God unless you repent and turn from your evil ways?
Do you not know that you are in the hands of God? Do you not know that he has all power, and at his great command the earth will be rolled together like a scroll?

Therefore repent and humble yourselves before him, or he will come out in justice against you. Otherwise, a remnant of the descendants of Jacob will go among you like a lion and tear you to pieces, and no one will be able to rescue you.

6.text: Now I finish my record about the destruction of my people, the Nephites. We marched out before the Lamanites.
I, Mormon, wrote a letter to the king of the Lamanites asking him to allow us to gather our people to the land of Cumorah, near a hill called Cumorah, where we could fight our final battle.
The king of the Lamanites granted my request.
We marched to the land of Cumorah and set up our camps around the hill Cumorah. It was in a land with many lakes, rivers, and springs. Here we hoped to gain an advantage over the Lamanites.
When three hundred and eighty-four years had passed, we had gathered in all the remaining survivors of our people to the land of Cumorah.

When we had gathered all our people together in the land of Cumorah, I, Mormon, was becoming old. Knowing this would be my people's last stand, and having been commanded by the Lord not to let the sacred records that had been handed down by our ancestors fall into the hands of the Lamanites (who would destroy them), I created this record from the plates of Nephi, and I hid all the records entrusted to me by the Lord in the hill Cumorah, except for these few plates which I gave to my son Moroni.
My people, with their wives and children, watched the Lamanite armies marching toward them. With the terrible fear of death that fills the hearts of all the wicked, they waited to face them.
The Lamanites came against us in battle, and every soul was filled with terror because of the vastness of their numbers.
They attacked my people with swords, bows, arrows, axes, and every kind of weapon of war.
My soldiers were cut down — all ten thousand who were with me. I fell wounded in the middle of the battle, but they passed by without killing me.

After they had gone through and cut down all my people except twenty-four of us (including my son Moroni), we survivors looked out the next morning from the top of the hill Cumorah, after the Lamanites had returned to their camps. We saw the ten thousand of my people who had been cut down, those I had led in the front.
We also saw the ten thousand of my people who had been led by my son Moroni.
The ten thousand of Gidgiddonah had fallen, and he had fallen with them.

Lamah had fallen with his ten thousand. Gilgal had fallen with his ten thousand. Limhah had fallen with his ten thousand. Jeneum had fallen with his ten thousand. Cumenihah, Moronihah, Antionum, Shiblom, Shem, and Josh had each fallen with their ten thousand.

Ten more commanders had fallen by the sword, each with their ten thousand. All my people had fallen, except for the twenty-four of us and a few who had escaped into the southern lands and a few who had gone over to the Lamanites. Their flesh, bones, and blood lay upon the face of the earth, left by those who killed them to decay and crumble and return to the ground.
My soul was torn with anguish over the death of my people, and I cried out:
O you beautiful ones, how could you have turned away from the ways of the Lord! O you beautiful ones, how could you have rejected that Jesus, who stood with open arms to receive you!
If you had not done this, you would not have fallen. But you have fallen, and I mourn your loss.
O you beautiful sons and daughters, you fathers and mothers, you husbands and wives, you beautiful ones, how could you have fallen!
But you are gone, and my sorrows cannot bring you back.

The day will soon come when your mortal bodies must put on immortality, and these bodies that are now decaying must become bodies that cannot decay. Then you must stand before the judgment seat of Christ, to be judged according to your works. If you are righteous, then you are blessed with your ancestors who have gone before you.

If only you had repented before this great destruction came upon you. But you are gone, and the Father, the Eternal Father of heaven, knows your condition. He will deal with you according to his justice and mercy.

7.text: Now I want to speak to the remnant of this people who are spared, if God will give them my words so they may know about the things of their ancestors. I speak to you, the remnant of the house of Israel, and these are the words I say:
Know that you are of the house of Israel.
Know that you must come to repentance, or you cannot be saved.
Know that you must lay down your weapons of war and take no more pleasure in bloodshed. Do not take them up again unless God commands you.

Know that you must come to the knowledge of your ancestors and repent of all your sins and wrongdoings. Believe in Jesus Christ, that he is the Son of God, that he was killed by the Jews, and that by the power of the Father he rose again, gaining the victory over the grave. In him, the sting of death is swallowed up.
He brings about the resurrection of the dead, through which all people must be raised to stand before his judgment seat.

He has brought about the redemption of the world. Whoever is found innocent before him at the day of judgment will be given the privilege of living in the presence of God in his kingdom, singing endless praises with the heavenly choirs to the Father, the Son, and the Holy Ghost, who are one God, in a state of happiness that has no end.

Therefore repent and be baptized in the name of Jesus. Take hold of the gospel of Christ, which will be set before you, not only in this record but also in the record that shall come to the Gentiles from the Jews, which record shall come from the Gentiles to you.

For this is written so that you may believe that record; and if you believe that, you will believe this also. And if you believe this, you will know about your ancestors and the marvelous works that were performed by the power of God among them.

You will also know that you are a remnant of the descendants of Jacob, and therefore you are numbered among the people of the first covenant. If you believe in Christ and are baptized, first with water and then with fire and with the Holy Ghost, following the example of our Savior as he has commanded us, it shall be well with you in the day of judgment. Amen.

8.text: I, Moroni, finish the record of my father, Mormon. I have only a few things to write, which my father commanded me to write.
After the great and terrible battle at Cumorah, the Nephites who had escaped into the southern lands were hunted by the Lamanites until they were all destroyed.

My father was also killed by them, and I alone remain to write the sad tale of my people's destruction. But they are gone, and I fulfill the commandment of my father. Whether they will kill me, I do not know.
Therefore I will write and hide up the records in the earth. Where I go does not matter.

My father made this record and wrote its purpose. I would write it too if I had room on the plates, but I do not. And I have no ore, for I am alone. My father was killed in battle, and all my relatives too. I have no friends and nowhere to go. How long the Lord will let me live, I do not know.
Four hundred years have now passed since the coming of our Lord and Savior.

The Lamanites have hunted my people, the Nephites, from city to city and from place to place until they are no more. Great has been their fall. Truly, the destruction of my people, the Nephites, has been great and terrible.

It is the hand of the Lord that has done this. The Lamanites are now at war with one another. The entire land is caught in an endless cycle of murder and bloodshed, and no one knows when the war will end.
Now I say no more about them, for there is no one left except Lamanites and robbers on the land.

No one knows the true God except the disciples of Jesus who remained in the land until the wickedness of the people grew so great that the Lord would not let them stay among the people. Whether they are still on the land, no one knows.
But my father and I have seen them, and they have ministered to us.
Whoever receives this record and does not condemn it because of its imperfections will learn of greater things than these. I am Moroni, and if it were possible, I would make all things known to you.
I finish speaking about this people. I am the son of Mormon, and my father was a descendant of Nephi.

I am the one who hides up this record for the Lord. The plates themselves have no monetary value, because the Lord has commanded that no one shall have them for profit. But the record written on them is of great worth, and whoever brings it to light will be blessed by the Lord.

No one can have power to bring it to light unless God gives that power, for God intends that it shall be done with the single purpose of bringing glory to him or benefiting the ancient and long-scattered covenant people of the Lord.

Blessed is the one who brings this to light, for it shall be brought out of darkness into light, according to the word of God. It shall be brought out of the earth and shall shine out of darkness, coming to the knowledge of the people. It shall be done by the power of God.

If there are faults in it, they are the faults of human beings. But we know of no fault. Nevertheless, God knows all things. Therefore, anyone who condemns it should beware, because they may be in danger of hell fire.
Anyone who says, Show me proof, or you will be punished — let that person beware, in case they are demanding something the Lord has forbidden.
For whoever judges hastily will be judged hastily in return. According to a person's works, so shall their reward be. Therefore, whoever strikes shall be struck by the Lord.
The scripture says: no one shall strike or judge, for judgment is mine, says the Lord, and vengeance is mine also, and I will repay.

Whoever breathes out anger and hostility against the work of the Lord and against the covenant people of the Lord, the house of Israel, and says, We will destroy the work of the Lord, and the Lord will not remember his covenant with the house of Israel — that person is in danger of being cut down and thrown into the fire.
For the eternal purposes of the Lord shall continue forward until all his promises are fulfilled.

Search the prophecies of Isaiah. I cannot write them all. But I say to you that the saints who have gone before me and possessed this land shall cry out — even from the dust they will cry to the Lord. As surely as the Lord lives, he will remember the covenant he made with them.

He knows their prayers were on behalf of their descendants. He knows their faith, because in his name they could move mountains, cause the earth to shake, make prisons crumble to the ground; and the fiery furnace could not harm them, nor could wild beasts or poisonous serpents, because of the power of his word.
Their prayers were also on behalf of the person the Lord would allow to bring these things to light.

No one should say these things will not come, for they surely will. The Lord has spoken it. Out of the earth they shall come, by the hand of the Lord, and nothing can stop it. It shall come in a day when people will say that miracles are over. It shall come as if someone were speaking from the dead.
It shall come in a day when the blood of saints cries out to the Lord because of secret conspiracies and the works of darkness.

It shall come in a day when the power of God is denied and churches become corrupt and are lifted up in the pride of their hearts — in a day when leaders of churches and teachers rise in pride, even envying those who belong to their churches.
It shall come in a day when there will be reports of fires, storms, and clouds of smoke in foreign lands.
There shall also be reports of wars, rumors of wars, and earthquakes in various places.

It shall come in a day when there will be great corruption on the face of the earth — murders, robberies, lying, deception, immorality, and every kind of evil. Many will say, Do this or do that, and it does not matter, for the Lord will support such people in the last day. But woe to them, for they are in a state of bitterness and bound by sin.
It shall come in a day when churches will be established that say: Come to me, and for your money you shall be forgiven of your sins.

O you wicked, corrupt, and stubborn people, why have you built up churches for your own profit? Why have you twisted the holy word of God so that you bring condemnation upon your souls? Look to the revelations of God, for the time is coming when all these things must be fulfilled.
The Lord has shown me great and marvelous things about what must soon happen, at the time when these things come forth among you.
I speak to you as if you were present, yet you are not. But Jesus Christ has shown you to me, and I know what you are doing.

I know that you walk in the pride of your hearts. There are only a few who do not lift themselves up in pride, wearing very fine clothing, and giving way to jealousy, conflict, hatred, persecution, and every kind of sin. Your churches, every one of them, have become corrupted because of the pride of your hearts.
For you love money, possessions, and fine clothing, and you love decorating your churches more than you love the poor, the needy, the sick, and the afflicted.

O you corrupt hypocrites and teachers who sell yourselves for things that will rust and rot — why have you corrupted the holy church of God? Why are you ashamed to take upon you the name of Christ? Why do you not realize that endless happiness is worth more than misery that never ends — all because of the praise of the world?
Why do you decorate yourselves with things that have no life, yet allow the hungry, needy, naked, sick, and afflicted to pass by you without even noticing them?

Why do you build up your secret schemes for profit, causing widows to mourn before the Lord, and orphans to mourn before the Lord, and the blood of their fathers and husbands to cry from the ground for vengeance upon your heads?
The sword of vengeance hangs over you. The time is coming soon when God will avenge the blood of the saints upon you, for he will not ignore their cries any longer.

9.text: Now I also speak to those who do not believe in Christ.

Will you believe on the day of your visitation — when the Lord comes, on that great day when the earth is rolled together like a scroll and the elements melt with intense heat — on that great day when you are brought to stand before the Lamb of God? Will you say then that there is no God?

Will you still deny Christ then? Can you look upon the Lamb of God? Do you think you could live with him knowing how guilty you are? Do you think you could be happy living with that holy Being, when your souls are tormented by the knowledge that you have violated his laws?
I say to you that you would be more miserable living with a holy and just God, knowing your sinfulness before him, than you would be living with the condemned souls in hell.
For when you are brought to see your unworthiness before God, and also the glory of God and the holiness of Jesus Christ, it will set an unquenchable fire burning within you.

O then, you unbelievers, turn to the Lord! Cry out to the Father with all your might in the name of Jesus, so that perhaps you may be found spotless, pure, beautiful, and clean, having been washed by the blood of the Lamb, on that great and last day.

And again I speak to you who deny the revelations of God and say they are done away with — that there are no revelations, no prophecies, no gifts, no healing, no speaking in tongues, and no interpretation of tongues.
I say to you that anyone who denies these things does not know the gospel of Christ. That person has not read the scriptures, or if they have, they do not understand them.
For do we not read that God is the same yesterday, today, and forever, and that in him there is no change at all?
Now, if you have imagined a god who changes and in whom there is any shadow of change, then you have imagined a god who is not a God of miracles.
But I will show you a God of miracles — the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. He is the same God who created the heavens and the earth and everything in them.
He created Adam, and through Adam came the fall of mankind. Because of the fall came Jesus Christ, the Father and the Son. And because of Jesus Christ came the redemption of mankind.

Because of the redemption that came through Jesus Christ, people are brought back into the presence of the Lord. This is how all people are redeemed: the death of Christ brings about the resurrection, which brings about a redemption from an endless sleep. From this sleep all people shall be awakened by the power of God when the trumpet sounds. They shall come forth, both small and great, and all shall stand before his bar, redeemed and freed from this eternal chain of death, which is a physical death.

Then comes the judgment of the Holy One upon them. Then comes the time when the person who is unclean shall remain unclean, and the person who is righteous shall remain righteous. The person who is happy shall remain happy, and the person who is unhappy shall remain unhappy.

Now, all of you who have imagined a god who can perform no miracles, I ask you: have all these things I have spoken of already happened? Has the end come yet? I say to you, No! God has not stopped being a God of miracles.
Are not the things God has done marvelous in our eyes? Who can even begin to understand the marvelous works of God?

Who shall say it was not a miracle that by his word the heavens and the earth came into being? And by the power of his word, mankind was created from the dust of the earth? And by the power of his word, miracles have been performed?
Who shall say that Jesus Christ did not perform many mighty miracles? And many mighty miracles were also performed by the hands of the apostles.

If miracles were performed then, why would God stop being a God of miracles and yet still be an unchangeable Being? I say to you, he does not change. If he did, he would stop being God. And he does not stop being God, and he is a God of miracles.
The reason he stops performing miracles among people is because they lose their faith, leave the right path, and do not know the God they should trust.

I say to you that whoever believes in Christ, without doubting, whatever they ask the Father in the name of Christ will be granted to them. This promise is for everyone, even to the ends of the earth.

For Jesus Christ, the Son of God, said to his disciples who would remain, and also to all his disciples, in the hearing of the multitude: Go into all the world and preach the gospel to every creature.
Whoever believes and is baptized shall be saved, but whoever does not believe shall be condemned.

These signs shall follow those who believe: in my name they shall cast out devils; they shall speak with new tongues; they shall handle serpents; and if they drink any deadly thing it shall not hurt them; they shall lay hands on the sick and they shall recover.
Whoever shall believe in my name, without doubting, to that person I will confirm all my words, even to the ends of the earth.

Now, who can stand against the works of the Lord? Who can deny his words? Who will rise up against the almighty power of the Lord? Who will look down on the works of the Lord? Who will look down on the children of Christ? All you who look down on the works of the Lord shall be astonished and perish.

O then do not look down on these things, and do not be astonished, but listen to the words of the Lord. Ask the Father in the name of Jesus for whatever things you need. Do not doubt, but believe. Begin as people did in ancient times and come to the Lord with all your heart. Work out your own salvation with fear and trembling before him.

Be wise during the days of your time on earth. Remove all uncleanness from yourselves. Do not ask God for things just to satisfy your selfish desires, but ask with unshakable determination that you will give in to no temptation, and that you will serve the true and living God.

Make sure you are not baptized unworthily. Make sure you do not partake of the sacrament of Christ unworthily. Do all things worthily, and do them in the name of Jesus Christ, the Son of the living God. If you do this and endure to the end, you will never be cast out.
I speak to you as though I were speaking from the dead, for I know that you will have my words.

Do not condemn me because of my imperfections, nor my father because of his, nor those who wrote before him. Instead, give thanks to God that he has revealed our imperfections to you, so that you may learn to be wiser than we have been.
We have written this record according to our knowledge, in characters we call reformed Egyptian, which have been passed down and changed by us according to our way of speaking.

If our plates had been large enough, we would have written in Hebrew. But we have also changed the Hebrew over time. If we could have written in Hebrew, you would have found no imperfections in our record.
But the Lord knows what we have written, and he knows that no other people knows our language. Because no other people knows our language, he has prepared a way to translate it.
These things are written so that we may be free from the guilt of our brethren who have fallen away in unbelief.
The things we have hoped for concerning our brethren — their restoration to the knowledge of Christ — are in line with the prayers of all the saints who have lived in this land.

May the Lord Jesus Christ grant that their prayers may be answered according to their faith. And may God the Father remember the covenant he has made with the house of Israel. And may he bless them forever, through faith in the name of Jesus Christ. Amen.
